compiled_state.title: [acli-integration]: test_add_comment_calls_acli_addComment_action and test_get_comments_calls_acli_getComments_action -> 2 failed assertions on clean main
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

**Scenario Type**: Pre-existing test failure (unrelated to recent session work)
**Source**: Discovered during /dso:end-session test gate run on 2026-04-27 worktree session

**Expected Behavior**: tests/scripts/test_acli_integration_comments.py passes the addComment and getComments assertions on origin/main.

**Actual Behavior**: 2 tests fail (assertion error) on a clean main checkout (verified via 'git stash' baseline). Failure exists pre-session and was never resolved by upstream commits during this session's window. The full-suite test gate run reported these as part of recorded 'failed' status.

**Failing tests**:
- tests/scripts/test_acli_integration_comments.py::test_add_comment_calls_acli_addComment_action
- tests/scripts/test_acli_integration_comments.py::test_get_comments_calls_acli_getComments_action

**Reproduction**: python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_acli_integration_comments.py on origin/main HEAD.

**Investigation Plan**: Run the failing tests with -v to capture the assertion message; identify which acli call signature has drifted; align test expectations with current acli implementation OR fix the implementation to match the contract.

comments.body: Fixed: tests checked for old ACLI v1.x syntax (--action addComment/getComments) but implementation uses ACLI v2.x subcommands (acli jira workitem comment create/list). Updated assertions to match the actual command structure.
comments.author: Test
